label: Uncomplicated tinea versicolor without systemic symptoms
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated tinea versicolor.
No secondary infection, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, systemic illness, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Cellulitis, abscess, pus, fever, systemic illness, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, mucosal lesions, or skin peeling/sloughing.
Immunocompromised host, extensive/recurrent rash needing individualized plan, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or dermatology if symptoms persist, recur often, spread, or the diagnosis is uncertain.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports tinea-versicolor framing as a common superficial fungal infection causing lighter or darker scaly patches. Autonomous Phase 1751-1800 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated tinea versicolor without infection, drug-rash warning signs, systemic illness, immunocompromise, extensive/recurrent individualized pathway, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports tinea-versicolor framing as a common superficial fungal infection causing lighter or darker scaly patches. Autonomous Phase 1751-1800 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated tinea versicolor without infection, drug-rash warning signs, systemic illness, immunocompromise, extensive/recurrent individualized pathway, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated tinea versicolor
no cellulitis, secondary infection, fever, systemic symptoms, or systemic illness documented
no drug-rash, immunocompromised, extensive/recurrent, pregnancy, pediatric, or specialist-directed pathway
